title: 10 best herbal creams for vomiting

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The leaves of Adiantum plants contain antioxidants that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to vomiting.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum extracts have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory activity, which may help alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting associated with inflammatory conditions.

Stomach-soothing properties: The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat digestive issues, including stomach upset and nausea, although the scientific evidence for these uses is limited.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with vomiting because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extracts from the Ginkgo biloba leaves have been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and soothing irritation. This can help to alleviate the discomfort and nausea associated with vomiting. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress may also contribute to its potential in reducing vomiting episodes and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Terpenoids: Specifically, ginkgolides and bilobalide, two types of terpenoids found in Ginkgo biloba, may help reduce vomiting by modulating neurotransmitter activity and exerting a sedative effect on the central nervous system.
Ginkgolide A: A type of terpenoid in Ginkgo biloba, ginkgolide A may help alleviate nausea and vomiting by blocking the action of certain neurotransmitters involved in the vomiting reflex.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Seeds: The seeds are also used due to their high concentration of ginkgotoxin, a compound that may have medicinal properties.
Barks: The barks are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100 grams of ginkgo biloba extract and 50 grams of emulsifying wax in a double boiler.
Melt the emulsifying wax in a water bath set at 60 degrees celsius for 15 minutes.
Add 200 grams of distilled water and 50 grams of vegetable glycerin to the melted wax mixture.
Stir in 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax into the mixture for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Irritant effect on the digestive tract: The menthol content in peppermint creams irritates the digestive tract, stimulating the production of saliva and bile to help move food through the digestive system and relieve nausea and vomiting.

Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds, such as menthone and menthyl acetate, relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to vomiting.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint creams, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by inflammatory conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with vomiting because of its natural digestive properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams helps to relax the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and discomfort. Additionally, the cooling sensation it provides can help to calm the digestive tract, reducing the urge to vomit. This makes peppermint creams a popular natural remedy for relieving symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, motion sickness, and other conditions that cause vomiting. Its effectiveness is attributed to its soothing and calming effects on the stomach.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: A terpene that helps with vomiting by reducing gastric contractions and alleviating nausea due to its anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties.
Menthol: A terpene that helps with vomiting by providing rapid relief from nausea and stomach cramps through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps with vomiting by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol and menthone, which provide a cooling effect to soothe digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: Contain oils that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea.
Stems: Similar to leaves, stems also contain menthol and menthone, which aid in relieving digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers and dry them for 2 weeks.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried mentha x piperita leaves with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours stirring occasionally to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then whip in 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Pour the mixture into small containers and refrigerate for 2 hours to set the cream.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

Isomaltose: A type of sugar found in licorice root, isomaltose can help slow down gastric emptying, reducing the frequency and intensity of vomiting by giving the body more time to digest food properly.

Flavonoids: Licorice creams often contain flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, ease nausea, and calm stomach upset.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with vomiting because of its anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on the digestive system. The root of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines. By calming the digestive system, these creams can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting, providing relief from digestive discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being. This natural remedy can be a valuable tool in managing vomiting and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which may help alleviate the underlying causes of vomiting and provide relief from its symptoms.
Flavonoids (specifically, licochalcone A): Licochalcone A has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and protective effects on the stomach lining, which may help alleviate stomach irritation and inflammation that can contribute to vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are commonly used to make creams for vomiting due to their ability to soothe the digestive system and alleviate nausea.
Barks: Glycyrrhiza glabra barks are used for their anti-inflammatory properties to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to vomiting.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are sometimes incorporated in creams for their potential to reduce stomach irritation and alleviate symptoms associated with vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 40c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 50g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 70c for 30 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil melt and blend together.
Stir the mixture until it thickens and cools to room temperature then transfer it to a glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Shogaol: Shogaol is a bioactive compound found in ginger that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic effects, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the activation of cholinergic receptors in the gut.

Gingerols: Gingerols are another type of bioactive compound found in ginger that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting such as pain and discomfort.

6-Gingergolic acid: 6-Gingergolic acid is a metabolite of shogaol that has been shown to have potent anti-emetic effects by blocking the activation of serotonin receptors in the brain, which are involved in nausea and vomiting.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with vomiting because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive system. Ginger has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by various factors such as motion sickness, morning sickness during pregnancy, and food poisoning. The active compound, gingerol, in ginger creams works by inhibiting the production of stomach acid and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby providing relief from vomiting and related discomfort.
remedy_constituents: 6-Shogaol: This ginger-derived compound has anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators and blocking the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in the vomiting reflex.

Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds, particularly 6-gingerol, have anti-emetic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the release of histamine and other pro-inflammatory mediators, and by blocking the action of serotonin, a neurotransmitter involved in the vomiting reflex.

Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene compound has anti-emetic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes, and by blocking the action of acetylcholine and serotonin, which are involved in the vomiting reflex.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high gingerol and shogaol content, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Roots: Roots are often employed for their ability to help alleviate nausea and vomiting, likely due to their similar composition to rhyzomes.
Buds: Buds may be used for their fragrance and potential medicinal properties, but their exact role in creams for vomiting is less clear.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250ml of distilled water with 15g of dried zingiber officinale root for 30 minutes at 80 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused water through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
In a separate bowl, whip 100g of shea butter with 50g of coconut oil until smooth.
Gradually add the infused water to the shea butter mixture and whip until well combined.
Pour the cream into glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach and digestive tract, reducing the severity and frequency of vomiting.

Carminative properties: The carminative properties of fennel cream help to expel trapped gas from the digestive system, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting, providing relief from these symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to vomiting, as well as alleviate discomfort and pain associated with it.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with vomiting because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The active ingredients, such as anethole and fenchone, help to soothe the stomach and reduce digestive discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with digestive issues, motion sickness, and even pregnancy. By promoting digestive health and reducing inflammation, fennel creams provide relief from vomiting and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds help alleviate vomiting by inhibiting the 5-HT3 receptor, a key player in the vomiting reflex.
Anethole: Anethole, a terpene compound, exhibits antiemetic properties by blocking the 5-HT3 receptor and has a direct effect on the gastrointestinal tract, reducing nausea and vomiting.
Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a sesquiterpene, also exhibits antiemetic properties, possibly by relaxing smooth muscle in the gastrointestinal tract and reducing spasms that can contribute to vomiting.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used to make creams for vomiting due to their carminative properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain essential oils that help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Seeds (again): The seeds are also used to make creams for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce discomfort and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare seeds, leaves, and roots from the plant in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare parts in a low-temperature dehydrator for 24 to 48 hours.
Combine 100g of dried foeniculum vulgare with 200g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture of foeniculum vulgare and oils in the double boiler for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the mixture and whip it with a hand mixer until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm cream helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to vomiting.

Anti-inflammatory properties: By reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines, lemon balm cream may help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with conditions such as gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties may help combat infections that can cause vomiting, such as food poisoning or viral gastroenteritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with vomiting because of its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The soothing compounds present in Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and eugenol, help to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. Additionally, the herb's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract can help to alleviate vomiting caused by stomach cramps, gas, and other digestive issues. As a natural remedy, Melissa officinalis creams can provide quick relief from vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid that helps alleviate nausea and vomiting by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing stress and anxiety-related symptoms.

Bitter principles (including verbascoside): A group of iridoid glycosides and phenylethanoid glycosides that work as natural emetics, stimulating the stomach and digestive system to reduce nausea and vomiting.
Caffeic acid: A polyphenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe stomach upset, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of vomiting and nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues.
Roots: They are used to create herbal remedies due to their antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections in the digestive system.
Flowers: They are used in creams for their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate nausea and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 4 hours to preserve them.
Combine 200 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes at 60 degrees celsius to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer the resulting infused oil into a mixing bowl.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of chamomile help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can cause vomiting.
Soothing and calming effect: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects on the nervous system can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for nausea and vomiting.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with vomiting because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin and luteolin in chamomile have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms in the stomach and intestines. This leads to a decrease in nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of chamomile help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, further alleviating vomiting and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid compound helps with vomiting by exerting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can alleviate gastrointestinal distress and reduce the frequency of vomiting episodes.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has a protective effect on the stomach lining and can help reduce inflammation, thereby decreasing the risk of vomiting caused by gastrointestinal irritation.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm the digestive system and reduce nausea and vomiting by alleviating stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high apigenin content, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate vomiting and digestive issues.
Leaves: They are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that contribute to their calming effects and help reduce nausea and vomiting.
Seeds: They are used for their sedative properties, which help to calm the nervous system and alleviate vomiting and related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and chop them into fine particles.
Mix 1 tablespoon of chopped matricaria chamomilla with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the mixture in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches 160-180 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture into a clean glass container and let it cool for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Inulin: Inulin, a prebiotic fiber found in dandelion roots, helps soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gut, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin found in dandelion flowers and leaves, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help combat underlying infections that contribute to vomiting.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in dandelion creams, such as sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes, can help calm the digestive system and reduce nausea by increasing the production of stomach acid and improving gut motility.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with vomiting because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains compounds such as taraxasterol and inulin, which have been traditionally used to calm digestive issues. By reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines, it can help alleviate nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties can also soothe the digestive tract, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from digestive problems.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin helps to alleviate vomiting by stabilizing the gut microbiome and promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate gastrointestinal issues.

Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin, taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce nausea and vomiting by protecting the stomach lining and alleviating inflammation in the digestive tract.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce nausea and vomiting by protecting the stomach lining and alleviating inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties in herbal creams and teas.
Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties in herbal creams and teas.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties in herbal teas and infusions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Clean and dry the harvested taraxacum officinale plant material in a shaded area for 2 hours.
Extract the herbal properties from the taraxacum officinale using a solvent like jojoba oil in a ratio of 1:10 for 2 hours.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with the herbal extract in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools, then fill into jars for storage and use within 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting by decreasing prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause stomach cramps and diarrhea.

Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can help protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to vomiting and other gastrointestinal issues.

Digestive stimulant: The bioactive compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting by reducing gastric stasis (slow movement of food through the digestive tract).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with vomiting because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has natural anti-nausea properties that can help alleviate vomiting. This is due to its ability to regulate digestive enzymes and reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can calm nausea and prevent vomiting. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to have a soothing effect on the stomach lining, promoting a sense of calm and reducing the frequency and severity of vomiting episodes.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound, curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and protecting against oxidative stress.

Tumerone: A sesquiterpene, tumerone has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which can help reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of vomiting.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A phenolic compound, DMC has been found to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, contain curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Stems: The stems of Curcuma longa are also used to extract curcumin, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain turmeric oil, which is used in creams to help soothe and calm digestive issues related to vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Extract 25 grams of dried curcuma longa rhizomes in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard the solids.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler until melted.
Combine the curcuma longa extract with the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm cream contains mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the stomach lining to soothe and calm irritation, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can contribute to vomiting.

Demulcent Properties: The demulcent properties of slippery elm cream create a soothing, protective coating on the mucous membranes, helping to ease digestion and alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and irritation.

Antioxidant Activity: Slippery elm cream's antioxidant activity helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to vomiting, and also soothes and protects the stomach lining.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, creams helps with vomiting because of its soothing and protective properties. The mucilages present in Ulmus rubra have anti-inflammatory and demulcent effects, which help to calm the digestive system and reduce irritation. By forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, Ulmus rubra creams can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting. Additionally, its soothing properties can help to calm the stomach, making it easier to manage vomiting episodes and promote overall digestive comfort.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: Triterpenes in Ulmus rubra cream may help alleviate vomiting by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate nausea.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids present in Ulmus rubra may help manage vomiting by acting as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory agents, thereby reducing the risk of infections and inflammation that can lead to nausea and vomiting.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids found in Ulmus rubra may contribute to the relief of vomiting by displaying antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Barks: Contain astringent properties which could help reduce vomiting by tightening the stomach lining.
Roots: May be used to treat digestive issues due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: Contain tannins which could help reduce inflammation and possibly alleviate vomiting symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of ulmus rubra bark and 20 grams of leaves by cutting them from mature trees in the morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to reduce moisture.
Combine 10 grams of dried ulmus rubra bark and 5 grams of dried leaves with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours and 30 minutes to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and allow it to cool before transferring it to a container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with vomiting because they provide a soothing and protective barrier for the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus. The gel-like texture of the cream forms a physical barrier that coats and shields the sensitive tissues from irritation, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with conditions such as acid reflux and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow cream contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, helping to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with vomiting.

Antispasmodic properties: The herbal compounds in marshmallow cream, such as flavonoids and saponins, have antispasmodic effects, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that may contribute to nausea and vomiting.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Marshmallow cream's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly from its polysaccharide content, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and nausea that accompany vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with vomiting.

Stems: The stems of the marshmallow plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Combine 100g of dried althaea officinalis leaves and flowers with 200ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture gently at 60°c for 30 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds from the herbs.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken overnight before whipping it with 20% beeswax and 10% coconut oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with vomiting because they contain the natural calming properties of valerian root, which soothe the stomach and intestines to alleviate nausea. The anti-inflammatory compounds in valerian cream reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, allowing for a smoother passage of food and reducing the likelihood of vomiting. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties help regulate digestion, calming stomach contractions and promoting a sense of relaxation, further decreasing the occurrence of vomiting.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties can help to reduce nausea and anxiety associated with vomiting, promoting relaxation and calmness.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian has antispasmodic properties that can help to relax the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to vomiting.
Gastroprotective effects: Valerian's gastroprotective properties may help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines, which can exacerbate vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of valerian is rich in sesquiterpenes, which are believed to be responsible for its calming effects on the digestive system, making it effective against vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal potency.
Combine 100 grams of chopped valeriana officinalis roots and leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 4-6 hours stirring occasionally to facilitate efficient extraction of valerenic acids.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix the filtered oil with 20% beeswax and 10% vitamin e oil then melt at 60-70°c for 15-20 minutes to create a smooth creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with vomiting because they contain bioactive compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as Dong quai, has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea. When applied topically as a cream, the angelica extract can help soothe an upset stomach and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, morning sickness, and other forms of vomiting. The calming effects on the digestive system allow for improved gut function, reducing the frequency and intensity of vomiting episodes.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Angelica's antispasmodic compounds help relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to nausea and vomiting.

Carminative property: The herb's carminative properties help relieve gas and bloating, which are common causes of vomiting, by stimulating digestion and promoting the elimination of excess gases from the digestive tract.

Anti-inflammatory property: Angelica's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting, as well as alleviate symptoms such as stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils, which help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting.
Rhizomes: Contains angelic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh angelica archangelica roots for a total of 30 grams.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 15 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler over medium heat.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes or until the wax has fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a glass jar, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with vomiting because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. The herb's natural oils also possess anti-nausea properties, calming the digestive system and easing symptoms of motion sickness, food poisoning, or pregnancy-related morning sickness. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, borage creams can help alleviate vomiting episodes, providing relief and comfort to those affected.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Borage cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.

Digestive Soothing Properties: The natural oils and fatty acids present in borage cream, like camolinic acid and linoleic acid, have been shown to soothe digestive issues and reduce spasms in the stomach and intestines, alleviating symptoms of vomiting.

Anti-Secretory Properties: Borage cream's anti-secretory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been found to decrease the production of stomach acids and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the borage plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with vomiting.
Seeds: Borage seeds contain a compound called gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers for a quantity of 250 grams.
Dry the herb in a warm oven at 50°c for four hours or air dry for seven days.
Combine dried herbs with carrier oils like sweet almond oil and coconut oil in a 10:20 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes to infuse the herbal properties.
Strain and filter the infused oil, then add beeswax and vitamin e oil before whipping into a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with vomiting because they contain isoflavones, particularly formononetin and biochanin A, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to nausea and vomiting. Additionally, red clover's natural astringent properties may help to dry up excess mucus and reduce congestion in the stomach, further alleviating symptoms of vomiting.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: These plant-based compounds help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to vomiting by soothing the stomach lining and reducing discomfort.
Saponins: Saponins have been shown to inhibit nausea and vomiting by interacting with the gut-brain axis and modulating neurotransmitters that regulate emesis (vomiting).
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), a common cause of vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids, which have anti-emetic properties, making them effective in alleviating nausea and vomiting.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and saponins, these flowers have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried red clover flowers trifolium pratense and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered red clover with 200ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20ml of beeswax in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Place the bowl over a double boiler and gently melt the coconut oil and beeswax mixture while stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of 50°c to 60°c, then stir in 20ml of vitamin e oil and 2% of lavender essential oil.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in an airtight container in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with vomiting because they contain the active compounds of the herb yarrow, which have been traditionally used to calm digestive issues. The creams' anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to vomiting. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial effects may help eliminate harmful bacteria from the gut, further alleviating symptoms. As a result, herbal yarrow creams can provide natural relief for individuals experiencing vomiting due to digestive issues or other conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting caused by inflammation.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in yarrow cream can help relax smooth muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms that can contribute to nausea and vomiting.

Anti-bacterial properties: Yarrow cream's antibacterial properties can help prevent bacterial overgrowth in the gut, which is often a contributing factor to nausea and vomiting, particularly in cases of food poisoning or gastroenteritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective in reducing inflammation and soothing stomach irritation caused by vomiting.
Seeds: Containing oil with carminative properties, seeds help alleviate nausea and relieve digestive discomfort associated with vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers then rinse them gently under running water.
Combine 250g of dried achillea millefolium with 500ml of olive oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Infuse the mixture by heating it to 60°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally then let it steep overnight.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Add 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil to the herbal oil mixture stirring until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with vomiting because of its unique blend of herbs that work together to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. The calming properties of lemon verbena, combined with the anti-inflammatory qualities of other ingredients, help to ease digestive discomfort and alleviate feelings of queasiness. Additionally, the cream's gentle, non-irritating texture allows for easy application to the skin, providing fast relief from vomiting and related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Essential Oils: Lemon verbena's essential oils, particularly citral and limonene, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining.

Aromatic Compounds: The aromatic compounds present in lemon verbena, such as linalool and beta-pinene, can exert a sedative effect on the stomach, helping to calm digestive disturbances and reduce symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

Flavonoids: Lemon verbena's flavonoid content, particularly hesperidin and naringenin, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the mucous membranes in the stomach from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing the frequency and severity of vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Buds: Containing high amounts of antioxidants, buds soothe stomach upset and alleviate vomiting symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems according to botanical guidelines for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 4-6 hours.
Grind the dried verbena officinalis into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix 200g of grated beeswax with 100ml of sweet almond oil, 20g of powdered verbena officinalis, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and store at room temperature for 24-48 hours before using or giving to someone experiencing vomiting symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for vomiting are topical preparations made from natural herbs that help alleviate nausea and vomiting by applying a soothing and calming effect on the skin. The best herbal creams for vomiting include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Foeniculum vulgare, Ginkgo biloba, Cinnamomum verum, Piper nigrum, Mentha x piperita, Aloe barbadensis, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, vomiting can lead to dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, and malnutrition, negatively impacting daily life, causing missed work, social isolation, and a decreased quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to vomiting that are treated with herbal creams are nausea, diarrhea, and constipation. Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint, ginger, and fennel are used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate discomfort. These creams can be applied topically to the abdomen or used as a suppository to provide fast relief from symptoms. They are especially useful for individuals who experience frequent episodes of nausea or have difficulty taking oral medication.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with vomiting is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Chamomile. Peppermint cream soothes the digestive system and calms nausea, while Ginger cream stimulates digestion and reduces inflammation. Chamomile cream promotes relaxation and eases stress, which can contribute to vomiting. Together, these creams provide a natural and effective remedy to alleviate vomiting and promote overall digestive health. Applying them topically can also help to reduce nausea and discomfort.

Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with vomiting because they contain anethole, a compound that exhibits carminative properties. This means it helps to release trapped gases in the digestive system, reducing nausea and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's natural antispasmodic properties can help to calm the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating cramps and spasms that often accompany vomiting. As a result, fennel creams can provide quick and effective relief from nausea and vomiting, making them a popular natural remedy for morning sickness and other digestive issues.

These compounds may help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, leading to a decrease in nausea and vomiting. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties of Cinnamomum verum help to calm the stomach muscles, reducing the likelihood of vomiting. Piper nigrum creams helps with vomiting because they contain the extract of black pepper, which has natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The presence of piperine in these creams stimulates digestion and helps to reduce nausea. It also aids in the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of vomiting. Aloe barbadensis creams helps with vomiting because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
